description: You are a high school social studies teacher at an OLPC-equipped school teaching a unit on the 1860 US Presidential Election. To help students visualize regional polarization and "swing states," create a Python script at /home/ga/Documents/analyze_1860.py that processes the popular vote dataset located at /home/ga/Documents/election_1860.csv (Headers: State, Lincoln, Douglas, Breckinridge, Bell). The script must identify states where the margin of victory was strictly less than 5.0%, calculated as ((Winner_Votes - Runner_up_Votes) / Total_Votes) * 100. Generate an HTML study guide saved to /home/ga/Documents/swing_states_1860.html containing a <table> element that lists these filtered states and their names.
